title: Chapter Sixty-Two: The Blood Flame Sect
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

They buried Won-chang in the earth outside the valley.

It was his own suggestion. The Great Turtle Breathing Method -- an assassin's technique, he called it, his family's secret art. It slowed the heartbeat, solidified qi flow, protected shattered meridians. He could lie underground for weeks and wake no worse than he went in.

Yeon-shin had not asked about the assassin part. Won-chang had offered it freely, and the strange relief in his eyes said enough.

"Don't worry about me," Won-chang said from the shallow pit, grinning up at them with dirt already freckling his cheeks. "This Desolate Divine Hero won't become anyone's burden."

Then he coughed, and blood flecked his lips, and Yeon-shin shovelled the earth over him without another word.

---

Clear Night Valley stretched before them.

The border of Chizhou and Huizhou -- named, they said, for some ancient master who lit the night sky with sword-light while fighting a demon. There was nothing noble about it now. The sunlight fell indifferently on bodies and broken ground, and the clash of weapons carried up the valley walls like distant thunder.

Hundreds of warriors formed a battle line that looked more like a war than a duel between sects. Red hair on one side. Blue cloth on the other.

The Namgung family.

From the ridge above the valley floor, Yeon-shin could see the family's command position -- elders in blue silk, a middle-aged man in pure white robes showing only his back, a handsome young man with gently downcast eyes and a drawn sword at his side. Blue Qilin Namgung Se-jin, the family's successor.

They were not fighting.

Fifteen days of battle, and the leadership had not moved. The Blood Flame Sect's Apostles watched from a distant peak, and whenever a Namgung elder stepped forward, the enemy sent swordmasters crashing into the battle line in retaliation. Hostages, in effect. Every family member on the front was leverage. The Namgung masters were paralysed by their own blood.

"We've arrived," Myo-hwa said.

Five red-haired figures approached from the valley mouth. Their leader -- shaved head, angular jaw, qi strong enough to brush Yeon-shin's skin from ten paces -- raised his hand.

"Leave everything you have and turn back."

They kept their distance. Properly cautious. These were not bandits.

"Desolate... blue...?" one muttered, eyes flicking to the robes.

"At that age? Must be born noble. Grew up eating spirit medicine like meals."

"Just need to overwhelm with technique. Truly excellent prey."

They began chattering, voices hungry. They valued absorbable qi more than their own lives. The Blood Flame Sect's true face.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa stepped forward.

Her palm struck the lead man's solar plexus. Azure Cloud Heaven Force -- the Zhongnan Sect's signature palm. The impact sent a shockwave tearing outward, shredding dust from the ground, scratching stone, hurling the others backward like dolls.

Yeon-shin moved through the aftermath.

He grabbed the shaved-head man's face with his left hand -- his fingers were long enough now, his body grown close to adult by the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique -- gripped, and ran.

The valley scenery scattered past in hundreds of lines. Wind roared in his ears. He drew his sword with his free hand, and sunlight shimmered silver along the wave patterns of the Northern Light blade.

He leaped.

Threw the man into the battle line below.

Landed.

And the sword song began.

It rose from the Northern Light like a living thing -- a sound no one on that battlefield had ever heard. Not a cry. Not a note. Something between, something that crawled into the bones and unravelled what it found there. The Blood Flame Sect members closest to him staggered, coughed blood, and fell.

He stamped the ground. The dust shredded. He ran, and the battlefield's flow twisted around him like water around a stone. An invisible typhoon with him at its centre. People falling in every direction. Most were instant deaths.

The refined chants spared the Namgung family. That much, at least, he had achieved in his days of meditation.

He penetrated the line and reached the Namgung family leadership in moments. Various emotions spread across their faces -- the family head turning from his white-robed stance, Se-jin's downcast eyes suddenly wide, the tanned young woman frozen mid-word.

That was when the familiar voices came from behind.

"Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Flash. You managed to return."

"The face that had lost its soul looks kind, but brought strange martial arts."

Two Blood Sand Swordmasters. Both with glossy black-and-red hair. Both smiling. Both holding thunderclap bombs with the easy grip of men who had thrown them a hundred times before.

Yeon-shin remembered them. The Dragon-Phoenix Gathering. The moment everything collapsed. His first retreat. His first shame.

He sheathed Northern Light and drew the Desolate Sword in one fluid motion. The cold of the hilt turned hot in his hand.

"You who throw first are the traitors," he said calmly. "Pay for your sins."

---
